articles.title: The Mythos Fable-5 Let Down 2026
articles.content:
这周最重要的两件事大概是外界对Fable的反应以及SpaceX的IPO。我一直在费力思考，谷歌I/O、微软Build大会以及苹果更近的WWDC 2026上，究竟有什么真正的新东西宣布。一种 palpable 的感觉是，它们许多技术 announcement 显得空洞，尤其在AI方面。我说不清原因，也许是AI新闻已让人疲劳，也许是 hype 被夸大，又或者是在LLM（如此多的模型）和更多数据中心投入三年后，我们仍未看到有意义的产物和应用层创新。我忍不住感觉，本期望生成式AI在2020年代能带来更多。 hopefully 到2027年，随着AI设备大量涌现，情况会不同。ChatGPT的Codex版本会让人感觉不同吗？据说，一位OpenAI高管曾直言“聊天已死”，将重心转向以智能体和编码工具为中心的平台。这恰恰说明ChatGPT的成功故事正在严重褪色。但此时OpenAI如何与Cursor或Anthropic在企业级市场（信任是关键因素）竞争？OpenAI可能不得不尝试降价，但他们已在大量烧钱。Cursor目前年经常性收入已达40亿美元，且增速更快。套用Sam Altman的话，肯定会有赢家和输家。他的眼扫描创业公司境况也没更好。超大规模企业的名字也会被AI改写吗？🤔 我曾在2025年6月写过：

关于巨头（ incumbent ）如何应对大AI（BigAI）的 verdict 仍不明朗？除谷歌外，其他几家看起来不妙。它们的AI资本支出与执行效果在多个案例中都显得相当疲软。我们先看2026年IPO结果如何，但苹果、微软、亚马逊等巨头感觉并未积极参与生成式AI前沿。苹果2027和2028年的AI设备阵容看起来相当 impressive，然而。一周前，消息称DeepSeek获得70亿美元融资，同时字节跳动和阿里巴巴大幅增加算力资本支出——中国正在全力投入。据彭博社周二报道，北京正计划在未来五年投入约2万亿元人民币（2954.3亿美元）在全国建设数据中心，以在加剧的AI竞赛中挑战美国。因此，国家发展和改革委员会（NDRC）正牵头制定蓝图，旨在到2028年将全国分散的计算设施整合成统一的国家网络，而非单纯依赖阿里巴巴、字节跳动或腾讯等私营科技巨头建设孤立云。如果中国感觉也必须这样做，那将是一个 brave new world。有趣的是，北京扶持华为的方式与特朗普政府通过生态系统的循环融资网络支持英伟达并无本质不同。但宏观来看，我们究竟在为什么文明建造？KDNuggets（非关联方）有一篇有趣的Anthropic Claude技能构建完整指南。我用来运营《Growth Unhinged》的Claude（内容）技能——如何用Claude AI进行投资（我们AI投资系列的首篇文章在X上获得大量收藏）。对Fable 5发布的X反应 conflicted 且相当 heated：🔥 同样让我 baffling 的是，考虑到Anthropic面临的法律、算力约束和IPO前压力。我想收集一些引语，为大家提供对该 announcement 和Claude Fable 5性质的 live-sentiment 反应。

“对Anthropic在AI开发任务上对Fable 5进行‘削弱’（sandbagging）存在大量 justified anger。但意想不到的副作用是，第三方评估者再也无法可信地使用该模型进行评估。”——Sayash Kapoor

“在我进行的一次次实验中，它以相当优势 basically 碾压了我用过的所有其他公开模型。它在众多问题上表现出色，并产生了一些惊人结果——它能执行多页规格达数十小时。”——Ethan Mollick

“Anthropic设计Mythos/Fable以阻止前沿AI研究是最令人失望的部分。希望这不会成为先例（我认为不会）。”——Ben Dickson

“Claude Fable 5是让我真正为软件工程未来感到害怕的最离谱模型。”——Menlo Ventures（Anthropic投资者）的Deedy Das。

“Claude Fable 5 改变了AI游戏。人们正在一蹴而就地制作游戏、3D世界、应用构建器和疯狂的代码优化。发生了重大转变。”——Min Choi

“当Fable 5用于前沿LLM开发时，它不会通知用户，而是通过提示修改、引导向量和PEFT等方法限制模型能力。”——用户@Hangsiin（据信是Jeong Seungwoo账号）。

“Mythos / Fable 令人难以置信。今天在客户电话会上，我让Claude在后台转录。当他们描述希望现有软件具备的功能时，Claude正在实时构建这些功能。”——Todd Saunders

“Claude Fable 5在医疗健康方面很可能 inherently 非常 capable。这很棒！可惜由于极其敏感的安全过滤器，几乎不可能利用这些能力。希望未来能调整。”——Tanishq Mathew Abraham

我真正不喜欢Fable的地方：
1. Anthropic收集我的提示历史，存储30天，随意处理。无法选择退出。
2. 他们可以随意削弱其最昂贵模型而不告知我，却按相同价格收费，浪费我时间。——Gergely Orosz

“fury 是真实的，也是我们开源社区多年来一直在说的，但普通人还没明白，因为对他们来说没有任何东西因“安全”被限制或剥夺。未来当AI融入生活方方面面，当它成为我们与世界的接口并了解我们所有 intimate 细节时，他们会非常 care。”——Daniel Jeffries

“实验室开始抽走扩散AI的梯子是不可避免的。不告知用户就这么做是错位的。”——Nathan Lambert

“在不通知用户的情况下降低ML研究性能是 shockingly hostile 且糟糕的公关。这可能 silently 损害各种工作，包括我的一些。这也可能引起全球反垄断执法者的注意。”——Dean W. Ball

“Fable是我用过最好的编码模型，遥遥领先。这是巨大飞跃，实现了更少提示和引导、更高效token使用、更优代码质量、更好工具使用、更智能自我验证、更长运行会话、更高信任与自主性。”——Boris Cherny

“Anthropic想控制谁获取模型访问权及用途，同时希望美国政府阻止中国实验室开发开源权重模型。抱歉，但***[不]（为粗俗用语编辑）。”——Zach Korman

“Anthropic Fable削弱最可怕之处不在于它拒绝回答生物学或密码学问题。而在于它预示了未来。一个由少数公司决定你能做什么不能做什么的世界。他们在建立新的统治阶层，而你不在其中……”——Péter Szilágyi

关于SpaceX IPO的分析在最后。领导力、AI政策、政府和风险管理的 intersection 在2026年看起来不妙。美国对闭源AI霸权的承诺也不显得 benevolent。Anthropic仍受算力限制并与五角大楼有法律纠纷，其最新模型明显受两者影响。许多AI研究者对Fable 5发布不满。他们距IPO还有五个月，是全球领先的编码AI模型和 likely 智能体AI执行者。这赋予他们做认为正确之事的权利。但 preaching 关于放缓或需要更多监管监督？现在说这个太晚了。Anthropic同时发布Claude Fable 5和Claude Mythos 5，标志着其下一代高自主性“Mythos-class”架构的公开 debut，但它也不受欢迎且被误解。Fable 5和Mythos 5定价为每百万输入token 10美元、每百万输出token 50美元——不到Claude Mythos Preview价格的一半。Anthropic表示，他们打算通过更广泛的“可信访问计划”扩大Mythos 5访问，用于防御性网络安全工作和生物医学研究。这些 safeguard （当Fable 5检测到涉及网络安全、生物学、化学和模型蒸馏的请求时，会将其转到Anthropic的Opus 4.8模型）初始调校更保守。Anthropic似乎正试图通过相当 intense 的 safeguard 构建护城河。其CEO最近又发表了一篇 essay：

他 essentially 呼吁更多政府监管，这有点虚伪，因为他的AI公司目前处于领先。他辩称AI能力正以指数级加速，与政府传统上缓慢、审慎的政策和立法方式造成紧迫不匹配。然而AI研究者指出，Fable 5的限制 also 过于严苛且非自愿。Fable 5发布后，许多人 renewed 对Anthropic作为“信任与安全实验室”却同时是B2B和企业产品层面AI前沿推动者的形象产生怀疑。其 incentives 已不再 aligned。

“对过去五年的任何合理分析都清楚表明，主要AI公司正在让世界变得更糟，而开源AI不是。所以他们用关于未来的假设性恐惧分散你对实际所做伤害的注意力。”——Stella Biderman

通过投射未来并相当意识形态化、政治化，且其CEO多次发表听起来像恐惧 mongering 的预测/警告，Anthropic在社区失去了 considerable 支持。这是我本周AI领域 biggest takeaways 之一。不幸的是，这种领导力缺失始于顶层。在这种气候下，AI注定会更政治化，同时处于可负担性危机中变得 inflation。这也降低了公众对AI的 sentiment。0:33秒。我很喜欢这句引语：Andrew Yang说，领导力的缺失让人们不得不自行处理AI相关问题。

Anthropic坚称Fable/Mythos模型在前沿，尤其在：
- 软件工程
- 知识工作
- 记忆与长上下文
- 视觉

明天是 big day。但人们会如何处理SpaceX及其问题？在其上周缩水路演中，公司表示股价135美元，预期市值1.77万亿美元。我读了许多SpaceX IPO文章，一些信息图吸引了我。分享一些我沿途发现的：

BigAI将筹集近3000亿美元。在 rampant 循环融资时代，马斯克的公司已在许多自我强化循环中运营。轨道数据中心、加速更多星舰发射、TeraFab和 scaling xAI 将耗费远超SpaceX和特斯拉拥有的资本和资本支出。到2030年代，我们将接近资本与债务限制被触达的时间点。SpaceX没有太多时间窗口。除Palantir、Arm Holdings、Snowflake和Airbnb外，大多数IPO定价过高。一年后平均下跌53%。

SpaceX在S-1文件中宣称的估值或总可寻址市场（TAM）已超出现实可能。无论是否为纸面万亿富翁，年长的埃隆·马斯克并不比他巅峰时期更出色。SpaceX用了20年才跨过2000亿美元估值，却在18个月内增加了接下来的1.5万亿美元。这听起来理性吗？它目前唯一有意义的收入来源是将算力租赁给Anthropic和谷歌等公司。同时持有SpaceX和Anthropic可观股份的Alphabet，面对如此估值，理应展现出些国家防务层面的相关性。🤔

xAI或许能实现规模化，但最大可能性是它能收购现已达到40亿美元年度经常性收入（ARR）的Cursor。SpaceX拥有以600亿美元全资收购Cursor的独家权利。计划是在SpaceX轰动性的公开募股完成后约30天敲定此事。但这不代表它必然发生。尽管背负大量债务并消耗巨额资本，xAI在B2B和B2C收入规模上均未取得成功。就2026年有意义的收入而言，SpaceX本质上并非AI公司。对公众而言，风险也是真实的。正是通过向Anthropic和谷歌出租算力，才使其2026年 projected ARR 预计显著高于2025年。因此，如今的SpaceX更像一家“新云服务”公司，而非火箭公司或互联公司。在这些基础设施交易前，SpaceX 2026年核心收入基线（发射业务+星链订阅）预计仅达220亿美元。但算力交易将其2026年预期收入提升至约340亿美元。这仍无法解释近2万亿美元的估值。蓝色起源火箭爆炸或许是IPO前最佳消息——某些贝索斯似乎没有 contingency plan。其位于佛罗里达的发射复合体36号（LC-36）被彻底摧毁，严重拖累了其星链竞争对手。唯一能让你合理认为xAI或SpaceX处于前沿的领域，是它们在算力上的投资（见下图）。为维持运营，它们可能不得不转型为新云服务。若与特斯拉合并，至少能获得能源和电池存储业务。SpaceX需要收入和资本来推进其“ moonshots”，而它们恰恰缺乏这些资金。正如其在公开亮相前最近的S-1注册文件中披露，截至2026年第一季度末，SpaceX持有约291亿至300亿美元总债务。关键的是，其中200亿美元为短期过桥贷款结构。IPO所得的大部分资金仅用于偿债。其750亿美元的IPO募资额所剩无几。仅2026年第一季度，SpaceX在AI相关资本支出上就高达77亿美元。xAI烧钱速度堪比ARR已达Anthropic或OpenAI水平的公司，但它并非如此。这是个问题。其他火箭公司如Rocket Lab在发射能力上正快速追赶。更多火箭公司正在涌现。这还不算中国在火箭发射和空间基础设施方面的能力。时间将证明，SpaceX是否会被（加速）批准提前登陆纳斯达克而得到支撑。当埃隆·马斯克的众多公司在一个它们已或即将不再是任何领域先行者赢家的世界中转型时，它们面临的工作任重道远。感谢阅读！
articles.summary:
We don’t really know what AI could become…

Good Morning,

The two biggest events this week are likely the reaction to Fable and the SpaceX IPO.

I’ve been struggling a little trying to think about what’s really new that was announced at Google I/O, Microsoft Build Conference and Apple’s own more recent developer conference of WWDC 2026. There was a tangible sense of how empty many of their tech announcements were, especially with regards to AI.

I can’t even put my finger on why this is, maybe it’s the AI overload of the news cycle, the exaggerated hype or how lacking in meaningful products and application layer innovations we’ve even seen after three years of LLMs (so many models) and more datacenters. I can’t help put have the feeling that I was expecting more in the 2020s that we are likely to see from Generative AI.

Hopefully next year in 2027 with a deluge of AI devices this will feel different. Will a Codex version of ChatGPT feel different? Apparently, a senior OpenAI executive went so far as to say that “chat is dead,” shifting its focus toward a platform centered on agents and coding tools. It just goes to show how ChatGPT’s success story is fading so badly. But how would OpenAI ever compete with Cursor or Anthropic at this point for Enterprises and companies where trust is a major factor. OpenAI might have to try to lower prices, but they are already burning so much capital. Cursor who now have $4 Bn. in ARR and are accelerating even faster.

There certainly will be winners and losers to paraphrase Sam Altman. His eye-scanning startup isn’t fairing any better.

Will BigAI Disrupt Incumbents by 2030?

Will the hyperscaler names be re-written by AI too? 🤔 I had written about this back in June, 2025:

The verdict on how BigTech (incumbents) react to BigAI is still unclear? Outside of Google, it doesn’t look so good for the others. Their capex to AI execution is looking rather poor in more than one case.

Let’s first see how the 2026 IPOs pan out but Apple, Microsoft, Amazon and other incumbents don’t feel like they are active participants at the frontier of Generative AI. Apple’s lineup of AI devices looks fairly impressive however for 2027 and 2028.

China’s Massive AI Compute Announcement

With news that DeepSeek is getting $7 Billion in funding a week ago along with massive Capex increases from ByteDance and Alibaba for compute - China is going all in. China is preparing to spend around 2 trillion yuan ($295.43 billion) over the next five years on building data centers ​across the country, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, as ‌Beijing looks to challenge the U.S. in the intensifying AI race.

So instead of relying purely on private tech giants like Alibaba, ByteDance or Tencent to build isolated clouds, the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) is spearheading a blueprint to knit the country's scattered computing facilities into a unified national network by 2028. If China feels they have to do this too, it’s going to be a brave new world. Curiously how Beijing is boosting Huawei isn’t so different how the Trump Administration has been supporting Nvidia with circular financing of the entire ecosystem in a spider-web of deals. But what exactly are we building for civilization in the big picture.

We Don’t really know what we are getting ourselves into…

Claude Skills Guides

  • KDNuggets (not an affiliate) has an interesting Anthropic’s Complete Guide to Claude Skills Building.

  • The Claude (Content) skills I use to run Growth Unhinged by

  • How to use Claude AI for Investing (the first post in our series on AI in Investing got a lot of bookmark saves on X).

Reactions to the Frontier Model of Anthropic

Reactions on X to the Fable 5 release were conflicted and fairly heated: 🔥 Also baffling to me considering the pressure Anthropic is under both from a legal, compute constraint and pre-IPO perspective.

Fable 5 is Controversial

I wanted to gather some quotes to give you a live-sentiment sort of reaction to the announcement and nature of Claude Fable 5.

Quotes:

“There is a lot of justified anger at Anthropic for sandbagging Fable 5 for AI development tasks. But an unanticipated side effect is that third-party evaluators can no longer credibly use the model for evaluations.” - Sayash Kapoor

“In experiment after experiment I conducted, it outperformed basically every other public model I have used by a considerable margin. It was capable across many problems and produced some startling results — it would work up to a dozen hours executing on multi-page specifications.” - Ethan Mollick

“Anthropic designing Mythos/Fable to prevent frontier AI research is the most disappointing part of their release. Hope it doesn't set a precedent (I don't think it will).” - Ben Dickson

“Claude Fable 5 is by far the most ridiculous model that makes me genuinely afraid for the future of software engineering.” - Deedy Das (Menlo Ventures who are investors in Anthropic).

“Claude Fable 5 just changed the AI game. People are one-shotting games, 3D worlds, app builders and insane code optimizations. There's a major shift.” - Min Choi

“When Fable 5 is used for frontier LLM development, it does not notify the user and instead limits the model’s capabilities through methods such as prompt modification, steering vectors, and PEFT.” - User @Hangsiin - this is thought to be the account of Seungwoo Jeong.

“Mythos / Fable is unbelievable. Was on a customer call today and had Claude transcribing in the background. As they were telling me about the features they wish their current software had, Claude was building the features in real time.” - Todd Saunders

“Claude Fable 5 is likely very capable inherently on healthcare. That's great! Too bad it's near impossible to tap into those capabilities due to their extremely sensitive safety filters. I hope this is adjusted going forward.” - Tanishq Mathew Abraham

Things I really dislike about Fable:

1. Anthropic collects my prompt history, stores it, and does whatever they want with it for 30 days. No opt-out

2. They can nerf their most expensive model without telling me, billing me the same amount, wasting my time. Whenever they want. - Gergely Orosz

“The fury is real and what all of us in the open community have been saying for years and yet regular folks don't get it yet because nothing they care about is restricted or taken away for "safety."

They will care a LOT in the future when AI is integrated into every aspect of our lives. When it's our interface to the world and knows every intimate detail about us.” - Daniel Jeffries

“Labs starting to pull up the ladders on the ability to diffuse AI was inevitable. Doing it without telling the user is misaligned.” - Nathan Lambert

“Degrading performance on ML research *without telling the user* is shockingly hostile and a terrible look. That could silently damage all sorts of work, including some of my own. Also the type of thing that could raise the eyebrows of antitrust enforcers worldwide.” - Dean W. Ball

“Fable is the best model I have used for coding, by a wide margin. It is a big step up, enabling less prompts and steers, more efficient token use, better code quality, better tool use, more intelligent self-verification, longer running sessions, and higher trust & autonomy.” - Boris Cherny

“Anthropic wants to control who gets access to their models and what they're allowed to do with them, but also wants the US government to block Chinese labs from developing open weight models. Sorry, but ***[no] (edited for profanity). - Zach Korman

“The scary part about Anthropic's Fable nerf is not that it refuses to answer biology or cryptography. It's that it foreshadows what's coming. A world where a couple companies decide what you can and cannot do. They're building a new ruling class and you're not in it...” - Péter Szilágyi

My SpaceX IPO analysis at the end. The intersection of leadership, AI policy, Government and risk-management is not looking good in 2026. America’s commitment to closed-model AI Supremacy is not looking benevolent either.

Watered Down Mythos 🌊

Anthropic remains compute constrained and in legal disputes with the Pentagon, and their latest model clearly suffers from both. Many AI researchers are not happy with the Fable 5 release. They are five months from an IPO and are the global leader in Coding AI models and likely Agentic AI execution. That earns them the right to do what they think is right. But preaching about slowing down or more regulatory oversight required? It’s way too late for that.

Anthropic’s simultaneous release of Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 marks the public debut of their next-generation, high-autonomy "Mythos-class" architecture, but it’s also not very popular and misunderstood.

  • Fable 5 and Mythos 5 are being offered at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens—less than half the price of Claude Mythos Preview.

  • Anthropic says that they intend to expand access to Mythos 5 through a broader trusted access program, both for defensive cybersecurity work and biomedical research.

  • The safeguards, which mean some queries on certain topics will instead be ported to Anthropic's Opus 4.8 model, are initially tuned to be more conservative. The safeguards are triggered when Fable 5 detects requests concerning cybersecurity, biology, chemistry and (model) distillation. Anthropic seems to be trying to build a moat via rather intense safeguards.

  • Anthropic’s CEO recently published yet another essay:

Read the Essay

He essentially is calling for more Government regulation which is a bit hypocritical since his AI company is the one that’s currently ahead. He argues AI capabilities are accelerating at an exponential pace, creating an urgent mismatch with the traditionally slow, deliberative nature of government policy and legislation. However AI researchers point out that restrictions on Fable 5 are also too restrictive and non-consensual. Many after the Fable 5 release expressed renewed skepticism in Anthropic’s image as being a trust & saftey lab that’s simultaneously the leader pushing the frontier of AI at the B2B and Enterprise product level. Its incentives are no longer aligned.

“Any reasonable analysis of the past five years makes it abundantly clear that major AI companies are making the world a worse place and open source AI isn’t. So they distract you from the actual harm they’re doing in the world with hypothetical fearmongering about the future.” - Stella Biderman

By projecting itself in the future and being fairly ideological, political and with a CEO who makes predictions/warnings on multiple occasions making statements/claims that sound like fear-mongering, Anthropic has lost considerable support in the community. This was one of the biggest takeaways this week in AI for me. Unfortunately this lack of leadership starts at the top. AI is bound to get more political in such a climate while being inflationary in the midst of an affordability crisis. This is also lowering the sentiment of the public towards AI.

We’re Missing the Boat on AI with a lack of Leadership ⛵

0:33 seconds. I really like this quote: Andrew Yang said that an absence in leadership has made it so the people are figuring out how to handle AI-related issues.

Anthropic’s Mythos Models are a Noticeable Step-up

Anthropic insists Fable/Mythos models are frontier especially in the following:

  1. Software engineering

  2. Knowledge work

  3. Memory and long-context

  4. Vision

SpaceX IPO Goes live on Friday

Tomorrow is the big day. But will the people figure out SpaceX and its issues? Heading into its abbreviated roadshow last week, the company said $135 is the share price and $1.77 trillion is the anticipated market cap.

I have been reading a lot of SpaceX IPO articles and some of the infographics really caught my attention. Let me share some I’ve found along the way:

The Price to Sales Ratio of SpaceX

IPOs will dump roughly $300 billion of new stock in the market

BigAI is going to raise nearly $300 Bn.

Space, Tesla and xAI Operate practically as one Entity

In an era of rampant circular financing, Elon Musk’s companies already operate in many self-reinforcing loops.

Orbital datacenters, ramping more Starship launches, TeraFab and scaling xAI will cost way more capital and Capex than SpaceX & Tesla possess. We will approach a time in the 2030s when capital & debt limits are reached. SpaceX doesn’t have much of a window.

Buying Most IPOs when they go public is a bad deal

Outside of Palantir, Arm Holdings, Snowflake and Airbnb, most IPOs are over-priced. Down on average 53% one year later. The SpaceX valuation or the TAM it claims in its S-1 is beyond possible. An older Elon Musk is not better than what he was in his prime, paper Trillionaire or not.

The Valuation is most like a Gigantic Ponzi-Scheme

SpaceX took 2 decades to cross a $200 billion valuation. It added the next $1.5 trillion in 18 months. Does that sound rational to you? The only meaningful revenue it’s now generating is leasing out compute to the likes of Anthropic and Google. Alphabet that owns a sizeable stake in SpaceX and also a sizeable stake in Anthropic.

SpaceX is valued as Big as all of Aerospace Stocks

It better deliver some National defense relevance with a valuation like that. 🤔

Will SpaceX even Acquire Anyspherer (Cursor)

xAI could scale, but most likely only if it was able to acquire Cursor, that has now reached $4 Bn, in ARR. SpaceX holds the exclusive right to fully acquire Cursor for $60 billion. The plan is to finalize this roughly 30 days after SpaceX wraps up its blockbuster public listing. That doesn’t mean it’s likely to happen. xAI has failed to achieve either B2B and B2B scale in terms of revenue, inspite of a lot of debt and burning huge amounts of capital. SpaceX isn’t really an AI company so far as meaningful revenue is concerned at least in 2026. The dangers to the public are also real.

SpaceX Hasn’t Scaled Revenue well Historically

It’s only renting out their compute to Anthropic and Google that saves their ARR projected in 2026 to be considerably higher than 2025. SpaceX is thus more of a Neo Cloud today, than a rocket company or a connectivity company.

Prior to these infrastructure deals, SpaceX’s baseline 2026 core revenue (Launch operations + Starlink subscriptions) was projected to hit just $22.0 billion. But the compute deals lifts its 2026 expected revenue to about $34 Bn. That still doesn’t explain a valuation of nearly $2 Tr. Blue Origin’s rocket blowing up was maybe the best news for the IPO. Some Bezos didn't have a contingency plan. Their Launch Complex 36 (LC-36) in Florida was totalled setting back their Starlink competitor significantly.

Will SpaceX just be a Neo Cloud?

The only area where you could legitimately think of xAI or SpaceX at the frontier is their investment in compute (see image below). So to make ends meet they might just have to become a Neo Cloud. If they merged with Tesla at least they would have the Energy and battery storage business. It takes revenue and capital for SpaceX to scale their moonshots, money they don’t have.

As disclosed in its recent S-1 registration filing ahead of its public debut, SpaceX holds approximately $29.1 billion to $30 billion in total debt as of the end of the first quarter of 2026. Crucially, $20 billion of SpaceX's debt is structured as a short-term bridge loan. A lot of the proceeds from the IPO just goes to paying off debt. Their $75 Bn. from IPO proceeds doesn’t go very far.

SpaceX spent a staggering $7.7 billion on AI-related CapEx in Q1 2026 alone. xAI is burning cash as if it was making ARR like Anthropic or OpenAI, but it’s not. That’s a problem. Other rocket companies like Rocket Lab are quickly catching up to its in the ability to do many rocket launches. More rocket companies are coming. That’s not counting China capabilities in rocket launches and space-infrastructure.

Time will tell if SpaceX will be propped up with the (fast-tracked) early entry into the NASDAQ. It has its work cut out for it as Elon Musk’s companies have to pivot in a world where they are or won’t soon be the first mover winners in much of anything.

Thanks for reading!
